title: Switchable chiral transport in charge-ordered kagome metal CsV3Sb5
type1_summary: No clearly identifiable cleaned, replot-ready tabular dataset was provided in the inspected file list.
type2_summary: No clearly identifiable raw/unprocessed measurement file was provided in the inspected file list.
classification_notes: The paper itself reports raw transport measurements and processed source/figure data, but the only inspected file is a PDF with no visible tabular structure. Without content extraction, it is not safe to label it Type 1 or Type 2. If this PDF is a Nature Source Data PDF, it may contain figure-linked tables, but that cannot be verified from the current inspection.

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper’s source-data deposit is described as figure/source data rather than raw instrument output, but the provided file inspection does not identify this PDF as a data table. No paper statement specifically ties this file to a raw measurement set.
file_classifications.file_evidence: The file is a PDF (2.3 MB), which strongly suggests a document or source-data appendix rather than directly replot-ready tabular data. No table structure, headers, or machine-readable columns are available from the inspection report.
file_classifications.reasoning: Based on the available evidence, this file is best treated as documentation/source-data PDF rather than a clean tabular dataset or raw instrument file. Because the content is not inspectable as structured data here, it cannot be confidently classified as Type 1 or Type 2. The conservative choice is documentation.
file_classifications.ambiguity: High. The filename resembles a Nature supplementary source-data file, but the inspection report only confirms PDF format and size, not whether it contains embedded tables or figures.
file_classifications.key_columns_or_structure: Not available from inspection; PDF document structure unknown

paper_analysis.summary: This paper reports strong, switchable electronic magnetochiral anisotropy (eMChA) in the centrosymmetric kagome metal CsV3Sb5, detected by low-frequency second-harmonic transport under an in-plane magnetic field. The effect turns on deep inside the charge-ordered phase, grows strongly below ~35 K, and reverses sign when the out-of-plane field component is reversed, linking chiral transport to spontaneous symmetry breaking, charge order, and likely time-reversal-symmetry breaking.
measurement_types: STM results are referenced from prior work, not newly measured here

figures.description: Schematic comparison of resistance in normal versus chiral conductors, showing the magnetochiral term proportional to B·I.
Illustration of nonlinear I-V behavior and second-harmonic voltage generation in a chiral conductor under magnetic field.
Cartoon of possible mechanisms for eMChA: structural chirality, chiral scattering, or electronic interaction-driven chirality.
Crystal symmetry schematic for CsV3Sb5, emphasizing that high-temperature structure is centrosymmetric and low-temperature electronic order breaks mirror symmetries.
Micrograph / device image of the low-strain microstructure fabricated by focused ion beam on a membrane spring.
Field dependence of second-harmonic voltage V2ω at low temperature, showing onset under near-in-plane magnetic field and approximately B^3 scaling.
Temperature dependence of antisymmetrized second-harmonic signal ΔV2ω compared with previously reported 4a0 charge-order intensity and anomalous Nernst effect.
Angular dependence of V2ω for current along a and c directions, showing a sharp sign reversal near the kagome-plane orientation.
Temperature-dependent angular scans of V2ω at fixed field, showing suppression of the effect at higher temperature.
Field-dependent angular scans of V2ω at fixed low temperature, showing strong angle sensitivity and sign reversal across the plane.
Extracted eMChA coefficient ΔR/(R|B||J|) versus field angle, with an inset of magnetoresistance versus field direction.
Field dependence of chiral conductivity Δσ at various angles, used to analyze the switching behavior.
Angular dependence of the first derivative ∂(Δσ)/∂B, compared with a theoretical model curve.
Conceptual sketch of chirality reversal near in-plane magnetic fields, with domain formation explaining enhanced eMChA.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper reports raw experimental measurements of transport voltages and resistivities on CsV3Sb5 devices: low-frequency a.c. current-driven first-harmonic and second-harmonic voltages, field sweeps, angle sweeps, and temperature sweeps. It also reports X-ray diffraction measurements used to characterize crystal orientation, and references DFT/tight-binding calculations as theoretical data generation rather than experimental raw data.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: The main figures plot processed transport quantities such as antisymmetrized second-harmonic voltage ΔV2ω = V2ω(18 T) - V2ω(-18 T), magnetoresistance ratios, extracted eMChA coefficients ΔR/(R|B||J|), and derived chiral conductivity Δσ and its field derivative. Several plots compare these processed quantities with previously published STM-derived 4a0 intensity and anomalous Nernst data.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states that data supporting the findings are deposited on Zenodo at https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.6787797. It also says that methods, additional references, Nature Research reporting summaries, source data, extended data, supplementary information, acknowledgements, peer review information, author contributions, competing interests, and data/code availability statements are available online.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw experimental data are not explicitly described as a downloadable file set in the text. The paper describes how they were collected: transport measurements on membrane-based devices with low-frequency a.c. current, magnetic field sweeps up to 18 T, angle-dependent rotation, temperature sweeps, and X-ray diffraction characterization. The exact file formats, folder structure, and whether raw traces are included in Zenodo are not specified in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include antisymmetrized second-harmonic signals, extracted eMChA coefficients, magnetoresistance ratios, and derived chiral conductivity. The paper also uses processed comparison data from prior literature, such as Fourier-transform intensity of the 4a0 charge-order vector and anomalous Nernst effect curves.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific data are implied by the presence of 'source data' and by the fact that the main plots are quantitative transport curves. However, the text does not enumerate per-figure source-data files or their formats. It is therefore likely that figure-level source data are provided, but the exact organization is unknown from the excerpt.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: The paper mentions theoretical calculations performed with Quantum ESPRESSO and a tight-binding calculation, but it does not mention analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories in the provided text. Code availability is referenced only generically through the online content statement.
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: The paper explicitly states that supporting data are deposited on Zenodo and that source data are available online, which suggests replotting is possible for the published figures. However, the excerpt does not specify whether the deposit contains raw traces, only processed figure data, or both.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is primarily transport-focused and figure-centric. The paper distinguishes raw measurements from derived quantities: V2ω is measured directly, while ΔV2ω, ΔR/(R|B||J|), and Δσ are processed/derived. Several key comparisons use external literature data rather than newly generated data. The Methods also describe how Joule-heating artifacts were checked and how strain was minimized, which is important for interpreting the dataset quality.

inspection_reports.note: PDF file, cannot inspect data content
